generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuationSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands into motor voltage via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller, adhering to ISO 15622 Performance Class I ACC standards for speed regulation without road curvature capabilities. Interfaced with PhysicalTwinEntity via ROS2 nodes (Actuation Functional Entity)."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Adjusts vehicle direction based on control signals from DigitalTwinEntity, interfaced via ROS2 nodes (Actuation Functional Entity)."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generates 3D point clouds for object detection and perception, feeding raw data into PointCloudClusteringAndTracking module via ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides supplementary visual data for object tracking, integrated with HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR for enhanced perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraVisualData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures and reports real-time vehicle state parameters including speed (m/s) and steering angle (degrees)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance HokuyoPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point clouds pre-processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering, transmitted via ROS2 IPC and Wi-Fi UDP/TCP to DigitalTwinEntity."
]

instance DepthCameraVisualData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "Supplementary visual data for object tracking, serialized and transmitted alongside LiDAR data to DigitalTwinEntity."
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state parameters (speed, steering angle) transmitted at 10-30Hz via ROS2 IPC and Wi-Fi communication."
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel
    base:desc "Processed object tracks (ID, position, velocity, size) updated at perception cycle frequency (~10-30Hz), transmitted to DigitalTwinEntity via DataTranslationFunctionalEntity."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements DBSCAN algorithm for LiDAR data segmentation and Kalman filter for object state estimation, processing raw point clouds into object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonEnabler
]

instance ActuationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands to motor voltage commands via linear mapping, interfaced with Vedder Electronic Speed Controller."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CPlusPlusEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object track data containing (ID, position, velocity, size) tuples updated at perception cycle frequency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance VehicleStateDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state data including speed (m/s) and steering angle (degrees) stored in SQL database for reporting."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides inter-process communication, node lifecycle management, and hardware abstraction for the Digital Twin System."
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Compute platform hosting ROS2 middleware, sensor fusion, and control logic execution for the Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel, ActuationModel
]

instance JetsonNXAntenna : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Communication interface enabling Wi-Fi-based UDP/TCP data transmission between Physical Twin and Digital Twin entities."
]

instance PythonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Used for rapid prototyping including DBSCAN clustering and data processing in the Digital Twin System."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel
]

instance CPlusPlusEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements ROS2 node lifecycle management and core control logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationModel
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Used for complex systems like point cloud processing and simulation-based offloading of ACC logic."
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stores historical vehicle state data and object tracks for reporting and external monitoring."
]

instance ROS2IPCEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Intra-entity communication protocol enabling data exchange within the Physical Twin and Digital Twin System."
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering), object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D Visualization User Interface (Presentation Functional Entity)."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateMonitoringInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes ACC logic (lead vehicle detection, speed calculation) on DigitalTwinEntity instead of resource-constrained ECU, reducing latency and computational load."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCComplianceAction, PerformanceOptimizationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows user-controlled ACC operations including enable/disable, desired speed adjustment, and emergency braking via Control Panel User Interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCActivationAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates historical reports for data analysis and external monitoring using SQL database queries."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceAlertInsight, DebuggingSupportInsight
]

instance SimulationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC logic execution to DigitalTwinEntity during development/testing, simulating resource-constrained ECU behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterCalibrationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time awareness of vehicle speed and steering angle for operator/driver monitoring."
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of detected objects in proximity with position, velocity, and size data."
]

instance ACCComplianceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensures ACC logic adheres to ISO 15622 Performance Class I standards for speed regulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insights derived from tuning ACC logic parameters like lead vehicle detection thresholds."
]

instance MaintenanceAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance alerts for potential system degradation (e.g., brake system) based on historical data analysis."
]

instance DebuggingSupportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data collection and reporting for identifying object track anomalies and system behavior issues."
]

instance ACCActivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-controlled activation/deactivation of ACC system functionality."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Safety-critical emergency braking command executed via user/operator intervention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterCalibrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calibration of system parameters like Kalman filter covariance matrices for improved tracking accuracy."
]

// C12: Functional Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hosts resource-specific functionality including sensors, actuators, and pre-processing (Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuationSystem, SteeringServo
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Executes control logic (ACC), data collection, and reporting, split into Controlling, Data Collecting, and Reporting Functional Entities."
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance ControllingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Implements ACC logic including lead vehicle detection and speed regulation."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance DataCollectingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Responsible for collecting and processing object track data and vehicle state information."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance ReportingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Generates reports and stores historical data in SQL database for external monitoring."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance PresentationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Provides 3D visualization of vehicle state and object tracks via User Interface."
    base:isContainedIn UserEntity
]

instance UserInterfaceFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles user-controlled ACC operations including enable/disable and speed adjustments."
    base:isContainedIn UserEntity
]

instance DataTranslationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages data serialization, unit conversion, and transmission between Physical Twin and Digital Twin entities."
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

// C13: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment hosting the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle and its associated Digital Twin System."
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity, DataTranslationFunctionalEntity
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Provides interfaces including 3D Visualization and Control Panel via Presentation and User Interface Functional Entities."
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The virtual-to-physical interaction includes:
  - **Control commands**: ACC-derived desired speed adjustments (Hz) and emergency braking signals sent to the PT’s **Actuation Functional Entity** via **ROS2 nodes**.
  - **Actuation signals**: Motor voltage commands (converted from desired speed) and steering servo adjustments.
  Commands are transmitted via **Wi-Fi network** (Data Translation Functional Entity) to the PT’s **Actuation Functional Entity**, which executes them on the **Vedder Electronic Speed Controller** and **steering servo**. The system ensures **clock synchronization (±100 ms)** between PT and DTS for coordinated actuation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates on a **real-time time scale** with the following specifications:
  - **End-to-end latency** between the vehicle and Digital Twin Entity is maintained at **≤100 ms**, compliant with **3GPP Technical Specification 22.185** for Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication.
  - **Clock synchronization** between components is accurate to **±100 ms**, achieved via distributed system protocols (e.g., **ROS2 time synchronization**).
  - **Data collection and processing** occur at **perception cycle frequencies** (e.g., **LiDAR data** at ~10 Hz, **camera data** at ~30 Hz), with **object tracking updates** at **~10 Hz** (Kalman filter rate).
  - **ACC control loop** operates at a **fixed update rate** (not explicitly stated but inferred to align with vehicle dynamics, typically **10–100 Hz** for real-time systems)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is structured as a **multi-entity, cross-layer architecture** with the following orchestration:
  1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensors (LiDAR, camera), actuators (motor/servo), and pre-processing (DBSCAN + Kalman filter).
  2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Executes ACC logic (Controlling FE), collects data (Data Collecting FE), and generates reports (Reporting FE). Split into **real-time** (timesensitive computations) and **offline** components (e.g., data analysis).
  3) **User Entity**: Provides **3D Visualization** (Presentation FE) and **Control Panel** (User Interface FE) via a unified UI.
  4) **Cross-System Entity**: Manages **Data Translation** (serialization, unit conversion, message alignment) between entities.
  The constellation adheres to **ISO 23247 entity-based RA** but introduces a **Physical Twin Entity** to encapsulate PT-specific resources. Communication flows via **ROS2 (intra-entity)** and **Wi-Fi (inter-entity)**. The system is designed for **Human-in-the-Loop (Liberté to act vs. Ability to act taxonomy)** with operators/developers as primary users."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin supports **operational and developmental lifecycle stages**:
  - **Design/Development**: Used as a **cyber-physical experimentation facility** for ACC logic validation and parameter tuning (e.g., Kalman filter calibration, DBSCAN thresholds).
  - **Operation**: Enables **real-time monitoring, remote management, and predictive maintenance** of the ACC system during vehicle operation.
  The DT represents the **as-operated state** of the physical twin, with models validated against real-world sensor data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, object tracks). No explicit 'as-manufactured' or 'as-designed' representations are mentioned in the documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Sensor Data Fidelity**: LiDAR/camera data validated against real-world object detection (e.g., object tracks updated at perception cycle frequency).
  - **Model Accuracy**: **Kalman filter** and **DBSCAN** parameters tuned via empirical testing on the PT.
  - **Latency Validation**: End-to-end latency tested against **3GPP TS 22.185** (<100 ms for V2V communication).
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Verified via **ROS2 clock synchronization** (±100 ms accuracy).
  - **ACC Compliance**: Validated against **ISO 15622 Performance Class I** standards for speed regulation.
  Uncertainty sources include **sensor noise** (LiDAR/camera) and **network jitter** (Wi-Fi communication), mitigated via **real-time filtering** and **hardware-based clock synchronization**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System employs the following technical connections:
  - **Intra-Entity Communication**: **ROS2 inter-process communication (IPC)** for intra-entity messaging (e.g., PT Entity → DTE).
  - **Inter-Entity Communication**: **Wi-Fi-based UDP/TCP** (Data Translation Functional Entity) for cross-entity data exchange (e.g., PT → DTE, DTE → PT).
  - **Network Protocols**: **ROS2 serialization** (for intra-entity) and **custom message formats** (for inter-entity, aligned via Data Translation FE).
  - **Latency Requirements**: **<100 ms end-to-end** (PT-to-DT-to-PT) as per **3GPP TS 22.185** for V2V communication.
  - **Clock Synchronization**: **ROS2 clock synchronization** (±100 ms) for temporal alignment of PT and DTS components."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System is deployed in a **distributed, hardware-agnostic architecture**:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosted on the **1/10-scale autonomous vehicle** (Jetson Orin Nano, ROS2).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Potentially hosted on **edge/cloud infrastructure** (not specified in documents) for offloading compute-intensive tasks (e.g., ACC logic).
  - **User Entity**: Hosted on a **remote workstation** (3D visualization, control panel).
  - **Data Storage**: External **SQL database** (off-vehicle) for historical data reporting.
  The system leverages **ROS2’s hardware abstraction** for portability across deployment environments. No explicit mention of **containerization** (e.g., Docker) or **orchestration tools** (e.g., Kubernetes) in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System integrates horizontally with:
  - **External Sensors/Actuators**: Not explicitly mentioned, but the system is designed for **modular expansion** via ROS2 (e.g., additional LiDAR/camera nodes).
  - **Simulation Tools**: Leverages **ROS2-based simulation** for ACC logic offloading (e.g., surrogate ECU testing). No explicit integration with third-party simulators (e.g., CREO Simulate) in this case study.
  - **Cloud Services**: Potential offloading of **Digital Twin Entity** components to cloud infrastructure (not implemented here but implied for scalability).
  - **Data Exchange Standards**: Uses **custom message formats** (serialized via ROS2/Data Translation FE) for interoperability. No mention of **OPC UA** or **DDS** in the documents."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy considerations in the documents are limited to:
  - **Data Storage**: Ego vehicle state and object track data stored in an **external SQL database** (owned by the DTS developers/operators).
  - **Access Control**: No explicit mention of **role-based access control (RBAC)** or **encryption** for data in transit/rest. The system assumes **trusted network** for Wi-Fi communication.
  - **Ethical Implications**: Not addressed in the documents. The PT is a **1/10-scale autonomous vehicle** (no human passengers), reducing safety/privacy risks. No discussion on **GDPR compliance** or **anonymization** of sensor data (e.g., object tracks)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System adheres to the following standards:
  - **ISO 23247**: Reference architecture (Part 2) for **entity-based DT design**, with deviations (e.g., Physical Twin Entity) to accommodate automotive needs.
  - **ISO 15622**: **Performance Class I ACC standards** for speed regulation logic.
  - **3GPP TS 22.185**: **Latency requirements** (<100 ms) for V2V communication.
  - **IEC 62541 (OPC UA)**: Not implemented but referenced as a potential alternative for **Data Translation Functional Entity** communication.
  - **ROS2**: De facto standard for **robotics/middleware** in the automotive domain.
  The system also follows **automotive best practices** (e.g., modularity, loose coupling) for system design."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  - **Safety**:
    - **Real-time constraints**: Enforced via **<100 ms latency** and **clock synchronization (±100 ms)** to prevent timing-related failures.
    - **ACC compliance**: Validated against **ISO 15622 Performance Class I** for safe speed regulation.
    - **Emergency braking**: Implemented via **remote user commands** (Control Panel UI) with direct actuation on the PT.
    - **Human-in-the-Loop**: Operators/developers retain control via **remote management** (e.g., ACC enable/disable).
  - **Security**:
    - **Network Security**: Assumes **trusted Wi-Fi network** for inter-entity communication (no mention of encryption or firewalls).
    - **Data Integrity**: Ensured via **ROS2 clock synchronization** and **time-stamped messaging** (Data Translation FE).
    - **Access Control**: Not explicitly addressed; UI-based commands (e.g., emergency braking) require **user authentication** (implied but not detailed).
    - **Safety-Critical Operations**: Remote actuation (e.g., braking) is **operator-initiated**, reducing risks of unauthorized control. No discussion on **cyber-physical attack mitigation** (e.g., adversarial input validation)."
]
